steps.title: Contrast scarcity with abundance
steps.content: The first sentence says biographers of pre-nineteenth-century subjects often lack accessible written sources. Marlowe's biography of Hamilton is different because he had a 26-volume, 19,000-document collection available.

steps.title: Reject agreement signals
steps.content: A would make Marlowe an example of source scarcity, B says his case follows the scarcity premise, and C presents abundance as unsurprising from scarcity. The transition must mark his case as the opposite extreme. The answer is D.
